document.title: Software Development Plan for G Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Software development plan for G Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector software, covering classification, development environment, requirements, risk management, verification and validation, release, configuration management, and support.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports regulatory technical documentation for software suitability, performance, safety, verification, validation, release, and configuration management of the G Series detector software.
keyPoints: The plan states that software is developed to manipulate and utilize the Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector functions according to intended use.
Software classification is addressed under FDA level of concern and EN 62304 software safety classes.
The software development process follows a classic waterfall methodology with analysis, planning, design, implementation, qualification, maintenance, release, and development plan update stages.
Software requirements include common functions, image acquisition, calibration, firmware update, firmware functions, cyber security, and error control.
Verification and validation are confirmed through software testing including unit test, integration, and system test.

Software release requires completed verification, evaluated results, documented residual abnormalities, documented release version, documented creation procedures and environment, and completed activities.

concepts.name: EN 62304 Software Safety Class
concepts.evidence: The software safety classes shall initially be assigned based on concern as follows: -. Class A: There is no injury nor health damage. -. Class B: The possible harm is non-serious injury. -. Class C: The possible harm is death or serious injury.

concepts.name: Software Risk Management
concepts.evidence: As for the software risk management, personnel who participated in the program development process shall decide, analyze potential risk, reason, probability and severity of the software.

concepts.name: Software Verification and Validation
concepts.evidence: Software verification and validation shall be confirmed through the software test. The software test shall conduct the unit test, integration and system test.

concepts.name: Software Configuration Management
concepts.evidence: In case the software configuration is changed, it should be validated before it is applied to the software.

evidence.claim: The document is a software development plan for the G Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Software Development Plan for G Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The report number and revision are A-TCF-10-143 (Rev.1).
evidence.evidenceText: Report No.: A-TCF-10-143 (Rev.1)

evidence.claim: Revision 1 is dated 2026-04-14.
evidence.evidenceText: Revision history Rev. 1 Revision date 2026.04.14 The reason & content for establishment and amendment Revision

evidence.claim: The document was prepared, reviewed, and approved on 2026-04-14.
evidence.evidenceText: Prepared R&D team Song, Jinsun 2026-04-14; Review R&D Manager Lee, Dongho 2026-04-14; Approval Quality Manager Hur Namkyu 2026-04-14

evidence.claim: The software development plan aims to develop software that can manipulate and utilize product functions according to intended use.
evidence.evidenceText: To use the Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector developed, produced and sold by this company according to intention, it aims at developing software that can manipulate and utilize the product function.

evidence.claim: Software is classified under FDA guidance into Major, Moderate, and Minor levels of concern.
evidence.evidenceText: S/W is classified into Major, Moderate and Minor by its level of concern

evidence.claim: Under EN 62304, software safety classes are Class A, Class B, and Class C based on possible harm.
evidence.evidenceText: Class A: There is no injury nor health damage. Class B: The possible harm is non-serious injury. Class C: The possible harm is death or serious injury.

evidence.claim: Until a software safety class is assigned, Class C requirements apply.
evidence.evidenceText: [If applicable] For each software system, until a software safety class is assigned, Class C requirements applied.

evidence.claim: The software development process follows classic waterfall methodology.
evidence.evidenceText: Software development process of H&abyz Co., Ltd. follows “Classic waterfall methodology"

evidence.claim: Hardware development resources identify the MCU function as a 32Bit Microprocessor and the MCU model as Intel Cyclone V.
evidence.evidenceText: Hardware resources Section Resource MCU Function 32Bit Microprocessor MCU Model Intel Cyclone V

evidence.claim: Software resources include Windows 11 64bit and Microsoft Visual Studio 2022 Professional.
evidence.evidenceText: Operation system Windows 11 64bit Compiler Microsoft Visual Studio 2022 Professional / aarch64-poky-linux-gcc / C# Debugging tool Microsoft Visual Studio 2022 Professional Editor tool Microsoft Visual Studio 2022 Professional

evidence.claim: Cyber security requirements include limiting random access by setting an ID and password for wireless network access.
evidence.evidenceText: Random access should be limited by setting ID and password when accessing the wireless network.

evidence.claim: Transmission data should be protected by encryption such as WPA, WPAPSK, WPA2, WPA2PSK, and WPAPSK+TKIP.
evidence.evidenceText: Transmission data should be protected by applying encryption such as WPA, WPAPSK, WPA2, WPA2PSK, WPAPSK+TKIP, etc.

evidence.claim: Verification activities reference EN ISO13485:2016 for software development planning.
evidence.evidenceText: Software development Devise a plan for the software development and put it in writing EN ISO13485:2016 Software development plan R&D manager

evidence.claim: Verification activities reference EN ISO14971:2019 for software risk analysis.
evidence.evidenceText: Software risk analysis Identification of the characteristics and hazards associated with the intended use and the safety of the device, risk assessment EN ISO14971:2019 Software Validation Report Quality manager

evidence.claim: The risk evaluation matrix rates severity and probability on scales from 1 to 5.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk evaluation matrix Severity Probability Not Entirely Harmful (1) Slight (2) Severe (3) Very Severe (4) Paralysis State(5) Frequent(5) Probable(4) Occasional(3) Remote(2) Improbable(1)

evidence.claim: If a hazard could arise from failure of the software system to behave as specified, probability is assumed to be 100 percent under EN 62304:2006/AC:2008, Clause 4.3 a).
evidence.evidenceText: If the HAZARD could arise from a failure of the SOFTWARE SYSTEM to behave as specified, the probability of such failure shall be assumed to be 100 percent [∵EN 62304:2006/AC:2008, Cl. 4.3 a)].

evidence.claim: Software verification and validation are confirmed through software testing including unit test, integration, and system test.
evidence.evidenceText: Software verification and validation shall be confirmed through the software test. The software test shall conduct the unit test, integration and system test.

evidence.claim: Traceability analysis is required between related documents and development activities.
evidence.evidenceText: As the traceability analysis is an essential element on comprehension of the effective product development, product design, development, testing and risk alleviation, do clear traceability analysis between the related document and development activities.

evidence.claim: Software release requires verification completion and evaluation before release.
evidence.evidenceText: It should be ensured that the software verification is completed and that the results are evaluated before releasing the software.

evidence.claim: All identified residual abnormalities should be documented and evaluated to ensure they do not cause unacceptable risks.
evidence.evidenceText: All identified residual abnormalities should be documented. In order to ensure that all identified residual abnormalities are not causing unacceptable risks, It should be ensured that such abnormal conditions have been evaluated.

evidence.claim: Software configuration changes should be recorded and managed in the Software Validation Report.
evidence.evidenceText: In case the software configuration is changed, it should be recorded, managed on 12. Software Configuration Management of the Software Validation Report.

evidence.claim: Software must be validated before configuration changes are applied.
evidence.evidenceText: In case the software configuration is changed, it should be validated before it is applied to the software.

evidence.claim: Software issues are recorded, corrective measures are conducted, and software is used only after required validation has completed.
evidence.evidenceText: Any issues identified with the software shall be recorded and applicable corrective measures shall be conducted. In addition, when correcting the issues of software, the company shall examine the issues, notify them to relevant personnel, and analyze any trend of issues. The software shall be used only after the required validation has completed.

reviewFlags.reason: Authority signal indicates MFDS/domestic in metadata, but the document body does not explicitly identify MFDS or a domestic Korean authority.
Document body identifies G Series but does not list specific product model names; model names in the source path were not treated as document body claims.
Several software safety class statements are marked '[If applicable]', so applicability to the specific G Series software is conditional.
The source text appears to contain translated wording and minor typographical errors, but key metadata and technical claims remain readable.
